mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in residential housing starts and a sharp drop in revolving consumer credit compress aggregate demand, forcing earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 850 and RSAFS declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative quarterly returns when both conditions are met, or single-proxy signals dominate the interaction effect in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvement, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates with negative equity returns during recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly proxies for corporate revenue expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings catch up to macroeconomic activity.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth shows zero correlation with forward equity returns over any 5-year rolling window, or earnings revisions decouple from macro growth trends.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading exceeding 4.0 percent annualized triggers inflation hedging flows and central bank tightening expectations, depressing equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the signal generates false breakouts in low-inflation regimes without subsequent mean reversion.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job growth acceleration and a declining unemployment rate confirm labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in the same month
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns when both conditions align, or single-proxy effects completely explain the interaction term in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 900 thousand units during a credit expansion regime signals robust consumer confidence and construction activity, lifting equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts above the threshold correlate with negative equity returns during credit booms, or the signal shows no incremental predictive power over GDP growth.
